item.title: Artemis II crew snaps historic Earthset photo on way home
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts snapped a stunning shot of Earthset — the moment when the Earth dips below the Moon’s horizon — on their long journey back home Tuesday after wrapping up a historic lunar flyby....

item.title: Jamaat-led alliance announces fresh protests from Apr 9
item.description: The Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami-led alliance on Tuesday announced fresh protest programmes, including leaflet distribution and holding protests and seminars, demanding the enforcement of the referendum verdict to implement the July National Charter....

item.title: JS passes 14 bills including ICT law
item.description: Jatiya Sangsad on Tuesday unanimously passed 14 bills, including the International Crimes (Tribunals) (Amendment) Bill 2026, which defines enforced disappearance as a crime....

item.title: JS passes 14 bills including ICT law
item.description: Jatiya Sangsad on Tuesday unanimously passed 14 bills, including the International Crimes (Tribunals) (Amendment) Bill 2026, which defines enforced disappearance as a crime....

item.title: US, Israel attack Iran infrastructure
item.description: US president Donald Trump warned ‘a whole civilisation will die’ in Iran if the country does not heed his midnight cut-off to open the Strait of Hormuz, as Tehran reported US-Israeli attacks on its infrastructure were already underway....

item.title: Partial internet shutdown in Manipur after unrest kills 4
item.description: A partial internet shutdown was in place in northeastern India’s troubled Manipur state on Tuesday after renewed violence caused the deaths of four people, including two children, officials said....

item.title: 1,236 measles hospitalisation in 24 hours
item.description: At least 10 children died from suspected measles and 1,236 others were infected across Bangladesh in the 24 hours until 8:00am Tuesday, marking the highest single-day infection since the government began counting cases past week....

item.title: India considers using reptiles along border
item.description: Field units of India’s Border Security Force have been directed to explore the feasibility of using reptiles such as snakes and crocodiles in riverine stretches along the Bangladesh border to prevent infiltration and criminal activity, The Hindu reported, citing an......

item.title: Banking sector faces negative CRAR for first time
item.description: Bangladesh’s banking sector has recorded a negative Capital to Risk-Weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR) for the first time, an incident rarely seen in functioning economies under modern regulatory frameworks......

item.title: 86pc entrepreneurs see capital shortage as major problem
item.description: About 85.89 per cent of 1.32 crore respondent economic units identified the shortage of capital as the main problem they face during economic activity in the country, according to the Economic Census 2024......

item.title: FM Khalilur on ‘goodwill visit’ to India
item.description: Foreign minister Khalilur Rahman on Tuesday afternoon left Dhaka on a ‘goodwill visit’ to India en route to Mauritius to join the Indian Ocean Conference scheduled for April 10-12....

item.title: Stocks extend upward trajectory
item.description: Stocks on Tuesday extended its upward trajectory, extending the momentum of last session, as renewed investor interest in beaten-down stocks pushed indices higher, while sentiment was buoyed by ceasefire talks surrounding the Middle East conflict......

item.title: Eastern Refinery can run production for 10 days with existing stock: BPC
item.description: The Bangladesh Petroleum Corporation has said that the country’s lone state-owned oil refinery, Eastern Refinery Limited, will be able to continue production for the next 10 days with its existing crude oil stock....

item.title: BB rolls out phased payout for depositors
item.description: The Bangladesh Bank on Tuesday announced a phased repayment plan for depositors of five banks merged into Sammilito Islami Bank, as protests by customers highlighted ongoing difficulties in accessing funds......

item.title: NBR signals possible hike in turnover tax
item.description: National Board of Revenue chairman Md Abdur Rahman Khan has indicated that the turnover tax rate could be raised to 2.5 per cent in the upcoming national budget, as authorities struggle with low compliance and widespread weaknesses in tax reporting......

item.title: Jet fuel price rises by Tk 25 a litre to Tk 227
item.description: The Bangladesh Energy Regulatory Commission on Tuesday raised price of jet fuel used in aircraft by about Tk 25 a litre following the energy crisis created by the Middle East conflict....

item.title: NBR, BB move to enable real-time invoice data sharing
item.description: The National Board of Revenue and the Bangladesh Bank on Tuesday jointly established an interconnection between the Customs Asycuda World system and the Foreign Exchange Transaction Management System (FxTMS) to facilitate real-time transmission of commercial invoice data through commercial banks......

item.title: Thai, Pak envoys talk trade with industries minister
item.description: Thailand on Tuesday expressed a strong interest in expanding its industrial and commercial ties with Bangladesh, highlighting the country’s rapid economic progress and attractive investment climate......

item.title: Queues for fuel oil get longer
item.description: Power, energy and mineral resources minister Iqbal Hassan Mahmud on Tuesday said that they were working on the price hikes of fuel oils in the next month while the high demand for the items amid unofficial rationing and supply shortage......

item.title: India moves closer to nuclear fuel self-reliance
item.description: India has moved closer to producing its own nuclear fuel after a domestic-designed reactor began a controlled nuclear reaction, an important step before it can start generating power......

item.title: Cargo handling grows at Ctg Port amid ME tensions
item.description: Chattogram Port has shown progress in container and cargo handling over the last three months, despite challenges such as Middle East tensions......

item.title: Power outlook bleak ahead of peak summer
item.description: Power demand has surged by more than 25 per cent in a month, triggering outages of three to four hours in rural areas just ahead of peak summer amid constraints on primary energy imports due to the war in the Gulf....

item.title: Strait of Hormuz shipping blockade update
item.description: Here are the latest key facts about impacts from the blockage of the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial shipping route virtually paralysed by the Middle East war......

item.title: Oil rises, global stocks fall as Trump’s Iran deadline looms
item.description: Oil prices jumped Tuesday and stocks fell after US-Israeli strikes on the key Iranian oil export terminal of Kharg island, with investors bracing for US president Donald Trump’s looming deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face devastating attacks......

item.title: Former DU prof Sumangal Barua dies
item.description: Professor Dr Sumangal Barua, a former senior teacher of the Pali and Buddhist studies department at Dhaka University, passed away in the capital due to old-age complications. He was 86....

item.title: Sri Lanka unveils $320m subsidy to offset energy costs
item.description: Sri Lanka announced on Tuesday a record $320 million relief package for farmers, fishermen and lowincome households hit hardest by soaring energy costs due to the Middle East war......

item.title: Mideast war drives Philippine inflation above 4pc
item.description: Philippine inflation jumped dramatically in March, government figures showed Tuesday, hitting a nearly two-year high of 4.1 per cent on the back of historically high fuel prices driven by the Middle East war......

item.title: Indian envoy meets DU VC, stresses academic cooperation
item.description: Indian High Commissioner to Bangladesh Pranay Verma paid a courtesy call on ABM Obaidul Islam, vice-chancellor of Dhaka University, on Monday to discuss expanding academic cooperation....

item.title: Australia, China agree to seek regional energy security
item.description: Australia and China agreed on Tuesday to back regional energy security as the world faced rising oil prices sparked by war in the Middle East, both governments said......

item.title: AI boom makes Samsung Electronics estimate record quarterly profit
item.description: Tech giant Samsung Electronics estimated on Tuesday that its first quarter profit had soared 755 per cent annually to a record high of 57.2 trillion won ($37.9 billion), driven by strong sales of chips crucial for artificial intelligence......

item.title: Pubali Bank, Square Hospitals launch new credit card
item.description: Pubali Bank PLC, in collaboration with Square Hospitals Ltd, inaugurated the ‘Co-Branded Credit Card’ at the auditorium of the hospitals in Dhaka on Tuesday......

item.title: HMC, SAML to promote financial literacy among ICAB students
item.description: Hossain Mumlook & Co, Chartered Accountants and Shanta Asset Management Limited have recently signed a memorandum of understanding to promote financial literacy, disciplined investment practices and long-term wealth creation among articled students......

item.title: Al-Arafah bank honours dedicated officers
item.description: Al-Arafah Islami Bank PLC, one of the leading Shariah-based banks in the country, has honoured its officials who have served the institution for more than 25 years in recognition of their long-standing dedication, integrity, and significant contributions to the bank......

item.title: Karmasangsthan Bank signs deal with Dutch-Bangla Bank
item.description: An agreement regarding collection of loan instalments and savings of Karmasangsthan Bank borrowers through digital service of Dutch-Bangla Bank PLC was signed on Tuesday, said a press release......

item.title: Shwapno extends support to underprivileged child Samia
item.description: Retail chain shop Shwapno has stepped forward to support 12-year-old underprivileged child Samia Akter from Pohnokucha West Para under Bagmara South Union No 2 in Lalmai Upazila, Cumilla, said a press release......

item.title: 36 antibiotics lose effectiveness, warn experts
item.description: Speakers at a World Health Day seminar on Tuesday warned that 36 widely used antibiotics have lost their effectiveness due to rising antimicrobial resistance, posing a serious threat to public health....

item.title: Community Bank hosts student banking confce in Tangail
item.description: The Student Banking Conference 2026 was held at the Zilla Shilpakala Academy in Tangail with enthusiastic participation from students and banking professionals......

item.title: Will China exploit US military engagement?
item.description: IN GEOPOLITICS, red line is not merely rhetorical threat. Taiwan’s possible declaration of independence is treated as the crossing of a red line by China. The fact that the Chinese government adheres to the One-China principle, clearly stating that Taiwan is a part of China. On the issue of Taiwan, the Chinese government is committed to achieving the complete......

item.title: Missing political civility signals comeback
item.description: BANGLADESH has made significant progress across various economic and social indicators since independence. But, it has fallen short of its desired development because of repeated disruption in democratic governance. Bangladesh has held 13 national elections — in 1973, 1979, 1986, 1988, 1991, 1996 in February and June, 2001, 2008, 2014, 2018, 2024 and 2026......

item.title: Choking on their own venom
item.description: DONALD Trump’s expletives-laced threats to Iran landed on Easter Sunday showcasing the abysmal vulgarity that his brand ofpoliticsincreasingly wraps itself in, aided and abetted by religious and racial violence. It was Pakistani scholar Ahmad Javaid who drew a distinction recently between religious zealots and liberals of his country. It’s difficult to find a bigot who......

item.title: Preparing Bangladesh for a renewable-driven grid
item.description: THE global energy system is undergoing a structural transformation unprecedented in scale and urgency. What was once a gradual shift towards cleaner fuels has now become an accelerated transition driven by climate imperatives, technological breakthroughs and geopolitical realities. At the centre of this transformation lies variable renewable energy......

item.title: Delhi should mind sorting out prickly issues with Dhaka
item.description: NEW Delhi has expressed its willingness to attend to the bilateral relations that have been constrained for quite some time now. This is so far welcome. Bangladesh’s foreign affairs minister visits New Delhi where he would discuss the issue of the extradition of the fallen Bangladesh prime minister Sheikh Hasina, who fled to India on August 5, 2024, amidst the July uprising and......Education resources Bangladesh

item.title: Naming defaulters is not enough
item.description: THE finance minister has named the top 20 entities that defaulted on loans in the parliament on April 6 in reply to a question of a National Citizen Party lawmaker for a Cumilla constituency. Ten of the entities are owned by the controversial S Alam Group. Two of the entities are owned by the Beximco Group, two by the Sikder Group, and one each by the Deshbandhu Group......

item.title: DU students stage protest against administrative negligence
item.description: A group of students under the banner of Dhaka University’s Conscious Students on Tuesday afternoon organised a symbolic training programme on ‘mail verification and call receiving’ in protest against administrative complications at Dhaka University that have recently led several students to......City & Local Guides

item.title: 3 members of bKash-Nagad fraud ring arrested
item.description: The District Detective Branch of police in a drive arrested three members of an inter-district money laundering and fraud ring early Tuesday in a fraud case filed with Aditmari police station in Lalmonirhat....

item.title: BNP appears to be following old path: roundtable
item.description: Experts at a roundtable on Tuesday observed that the Bangladesh Nationalist Party appeared to be repeating oldpoliticalpatterns, raising concerns over democratic governance and accountability....

item.title: DR Congo makes deal with US to take in deported foreigners
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo has said it will take in foreigners deported from the United States under a deal with Washington, which also wants access to Congolese mineral wealth....

item.title: Doctors in England go on strike for 15th time in 3 years
item.description: Doctors in England walked out for the 15th time in just over three years Tuesday in an increasingly bitter dispute with the government over pay and jobs....

item.title: 180 dead or missing in Med since March 28: UN
item.description: More than 180 people are feared dead or missing in Mediterranean shipwrecks over the past 10 days, the United Nations said Tuesday, with nearly 1,000 deaths counted since the start of 2026....

item.title: 12 killed in fresh Ukraine, Russia strikes
item.description: Russian artillery and aerial attacks killed nine people in Ukraine — including a child — in strikes across the country on Tuesday, officials said....

item.title: New version of Bangladesh National Portal launched
item.description: Aspire to Innovate programme, also known as a2i, the flagship project for the government’s Digital Bangladesh agenda, has launched the second version of the Bangladesh National Portal to make the government information management more modern, dynamic and secure....

item.title: Coast guard detains 2 with 15,000 Yaba at Teknaf
item.description: Members of the Bangladesh Coast Guard arrested two suspected drug traffickers with 15,000 Yaba tablets worth about Tk 75 lakh during a drive in Teknaf of Cox’s Bazar on Monday....

item.title: IU teachers, students stage demo demanding justice
item.description: TEACHERS and students of Islamic University staged a silent protest and mourning procession on the campus in Kushtia on Tuesday, demanding swift and impartial justice in the murder of the university’s social welfare department chairperson Asma Sadia Runa....

item.title: Pedestrian fatalities dominate Dhaka road deaths: report
item.description: About 56 per cent of all fatal victims of road accidents occurred in the capital in 2022 and 2023 were pedestrians, a report published on Tuesday said....

item.description: Bangladesh Coast Guard personnel seized around 4,000 litres of illegally transported diesel and arrested a man in Bhola on Monday....Human rights reports

item.description: A man was killed after being run over by a train in Lohagara upazila of Narail Tuesday morning....

item.description: Human health cannot be safeguarded without protecting soil and trees, said internationally renowned soil and environmental scientist and vice-chancellor of Gazipur Agricultural University, Professor Dr GKM Mustafizur Rahman....

item.title: JnUCSU demands maternity leave for female students
item.description: Leaders of the Jagannath University Central Students’ Union have called for the maternity leave policy for female students, citing the need for a more inclusive and supportive academic environment....

item.title: Feasibility study under way for Tk 3,500cr farm-access roads in haor
item.description: Feasibility studies were under way for a Tk 3,500 crore project to develop farm-access road communication networks in the north-eastern haor region, state minister for local government Mir Shahe Alam said on Tuesday....

item.title: Aminul claims himself only legitimate BCB president, terms Tamim illegal
item.description: Recently removed Bangladesh Cricket Board president Aminul Islam claimed himself as the ‘only legitimate president' while calling upon the International Cricket Council to ‘immediately look into this matter' after the National Sports Council dissolved his board on Tuesday. ...

item.description: The three-day ‘Bangladesh Economics Summit’ began at Dhaka University on Tuesday at the university’s professor Muzaffar Ahmed Chowdhury auditorium....City & Local Guides

item.title: 11 more illegal footpath shops fined in DMP drive
